footer.leadParagraph: Pepvise reviews the published peptide literature the way Wirecutter reviews mattresses — every entry ranked, every score on the same yardstick, no vendor links, no dosing prescriptions.
footer.footerNote: Pepvise reviews are not medical advice. We do not sell peptides, take peptide-vendor sponsorship, or recommend a compound for any individual. Talk to a licensed physician working within FDA-compliant pathways before any administration.

hero.lede: Every compound scored on the same five dimensions — published evidence, mechanism plausibility, human data, vendor trust, regulatory posture — and ranked. Last database refresh {date}.

rankIndex.heading: Our picks across the research-peptide category.
rankIndex.caption: Twelve compounds in active rotation, scored on a 10-point composite. Updated quarterly.

comparison.heading: Top four on every dimension.
comparison.caption: Higher is better. Numbers are tabular, the methodology is on the methodology page, and yes — vendor trust counts because counterfeit risk is real.

categories.heading: Five shelves. Every review fits one.
categories.caption: Each shelf carries the reviews and the comparison tables for a single research domain.

methodology.heading: Five dimensions, weighted, on every review.
methodology.caption: Every score on Pepvise breaks down into the same five components. Read the full methodology if you want to argue with the weights — we will hear you out.

items.label: Published evidence
items.body: Sample size, replication count, and study design across PubMed, ClinicalTrials.gov, and FDA filings. Mouse-only literature gets capped.

items.label: Mechanism plausibility
items.body: Whether the proposed mechanism is consistent with established cell biology, or whether it is back-fitted to the marketing claim.

items.label: Human data
items.body: Trial phase, sample size, follow-up length, and the gap between rodent dose and the human dose actually studied.

items.label: Vendor trust
items.body: Counterfeit incidence, third-party assay availability, regulatory posture of typical sources. Not an endorsement of any vendor.

items.label: Regulatory posture
items.body: FDA approval status, 503A/503B compoundability, WADA status, and the realistic legal pathway for a US patient or athlete.

pipeline.heading: On the pipeline.
pipeline.caption: Twelve reviews live, eight more in active drafting. Here is what is coming and when.

newsletter.heading: One review a week. No vendor links. Ever.
newsletter.caption: When a new compound enters the database — or when an existing review gets a score change — subscribers hear about it first.
newsletter.consent: By subscribing you accept the privacy policy. One-click unsubscribe.

alternatives.heading: Alternatives we tested
alternatives.caption: Three compounds that came up in the same comparison set.

methodologyPage.lede: Pepvise grades every research peptide on a 10-point composite drawn from five weighted components. The weights are public, the inputs are public, and the version history is public — that is the only way a score system survives contact with smart readers.

methodologyV12.lede: Released {date}. Tightens the human-data weighting after Reta-1 and Surv-3 publication, raises the vendor-trust ceiling, and removes the legacy WADA bonus that double-counted regulatory posture.

pipelinePage.lede: Reviews currently in drafting, in third-party review, or in pre-publication hold. Pepvise publishes when the citation pack is complete and the methodology lands at v1.2 or later — not before.
